name: Screen Shake
description: A temporary camera offset or rotation effect used to emphasize impact, danger, or scale.
design_notes: Screen shake is presentation, not a substitute for clear game-state feedback. It should be authored with intensity caps, decay behavior, priority, and user controls. The best shake supports readability by emphasizing events while avoiding motion that hides hazards, reticles, UI, or player position.
design_purpose: Sell impact and weight for attacks, explosions, landings, and world events.
Communicate danger or scale without changing simulation rules.
Differentiate small, medium, and major impacts through authored response profiles.
player_fantasy: The player feels the force of an event through the camera while still retaining control and orientation.

parameters.name: trauma_add_per_event
parameters.description: Contribution added to the current shake accumulator.

parameters.name: trauma_decay_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which accumulated shake falls back to zero.

parameters.name: max_positional_offset_pixels
parameters.description: Maximum screen-space offset applied at full intensity.

parameters.name: max_rotational_offset_degrees
parameters.description: Maximum roll, yaw, or pitch perturbation.

parameters.name: shake_frequency_hz
parameters.description: Noise sampling rate or oscillation frequency for the shake profile.

edge_cases: Repeated rapid events should accumulate through a cap rather than stacking into unreadable camera motion.
Cutscenes, menus, split screen, photo mode, and dialogue cameras need opt-in or suppression rules.
Shake must not move UI reticles or hit markers unless the game intentionally wants diegetic instability.
Large world coordinates should not introduce floating-point jitter into shake offsets.
common_bugs: Shake is applied before camera collision and pushes the camera through walls.
Multiple systems write camera offsets and one cancels the other each frame.
Pause stops decay but not event accumulation, causing a burst when unpaused.
The accessibility intensity slider scales visuals but not controller vibration or audio rumble.
balancing_notes: Use small profiles frequently and reserve large profiles for rare events so shake keeps meaning.
Camera motion should never be required to understand damage, direction, or timing.
accessibility_notes: Expose intensity and disable options, and pair impacts with non-motion cues such as hit flash, sound, or haptics.
Avoid high-frequency shake patterns that can cause discomfort or obscure precision tasks.

implementation_notes.unity: Apply shake as a late camera offset after follow smoothing but before final composition; with Cinemachine, use impulse profiles and scale them through accessibility settings.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep a shake accumulator on the Camera2D or Camera3D rig and sample deterministic noise in _process after the target position is resolved.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Camera Shake Sources or PlayerCameraManager modifiers with authored profiles and expose user scaling through settings.
implementation_notes.web: Apply shake to the render camera transform or canvas view matrix after simulation; keep UI layers separate when reticles must stay stable.

example_games.title: Nuclear Throne
example_games.note: Uses frequent shake to emphasize weapon impact.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses restrained shake to support impacts and transitions.
